name: El Puente de Segade — circular by the Embalse de Caldas and río Umia
name_original: El Puente de Segade — circular por el Embalse de Caldas y río Umia
region_intro: Hiking around Caldas De Reis offers diverse natural landscapes shaped by the Umia and Bermaña rivers. The region features lush forests, natural pools, and the Fervenza de Segade waterfall, which is a notable natural feature. Monumental rock formations like Pedra Filga and Pedra da Grada also showcase the area's geological history. The municipality is a significant stop on the Portuguese Way of the Camino de Santiago, providing varied terrains for hikers. Best hiking trails around Caldas De Reis The most popular hiking route is El Puente de Segade — circular by the Embalse de Caldas and río Umia, a 13.5 miles (21.8 km) difficult trail that takes 5 hours 55 minutes to complete. This route explores the Umia River and the Embalse de Caldas. Another top favourite among local hikers is Thermal Washhouse – View of Segade Waterfall loop from Caldas de Reis, an easy 3.9 miles (6.2 km) path. This trail leads to the Fervenza de Segade waterfall and passes by thermal washhouses. Local hikers also love the Roman Bridge over the Bermaña River – Caldas de Reis Bridge loop from Caldas, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail leading through the Bermaña River area, often completed in about 1 hour 52 minutes. Hiking around Caldas De Reis is defined by the Umia and Bermaña rivers, lush forests, and the Fervenza de Segade waterfall. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Caldas De Reis are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 370 reviews. More than 2,600 hikers have used komoot to explore Caldas De Reis's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find in Caldas de Reis?
A: Caldas de Reis offers a diverse range of hiking trails, from easy strolls along riverbanks to more challenging routes. The landscape is shaped by the Umia and Bermaña rivers, featuring lush forests, natural pools, and significant waterfalls. You'll find paths suitable for various fitness levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult options.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking routes in Caldas de Reis?
A: Yes, Caldas de Reis has several easy routes perfect for families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5340470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Thermal Washhouse – View of Segade Waterfall loop from Caldas de Reis&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 6.2 km path that leads to the Fervenza de Segade waterfall and passes by historical thermal washhouses. Another gentle walk is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31441710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge over the Bermaña River – Caldas de Reis Bridge loop from Caldas&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.9 km long and explores the Bermaña River area.

Q: Can I find hiking trails that feature waterfalls in Caldas de Reis?
A: Absolutely! The region is known for its beautiful waterfalls, particularly the Fervenza de Segade on the Umia River. You can explore this natural wonder on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38237395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Segade – View of Segade Waterfall loop from Caldas de Reis&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 7.07 km hike. The impressive Barosa Waterfall and Mills are also a notable natural monument nearby, which you can learn more about &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2030121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;here&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in Caldas de Reis are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For a moderate circular option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24611685" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Pilgrims' Path Section – Forest Path Near Vineyard loop from Caldas de Reis&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 11.5 km. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5340470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Thermal Washhouse – View of Segade Waterfall loop from Caldas de Reis&lt;/a&gt; is another popular easy circular route.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or natural features to see while hiking?
A: Beyond the stunning Fervenza de Segade waterfall, hikers can discover monumental rock formations like Pedra Filga and Pedra da Grada. The area also features historical elements such as old bridges, including one of Roman origin, and ancient millers' itineraries along the Umia River. For a cultural experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4464057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caldas de Reis Thermal Baths&lt;/a&gt; are a significant historical site, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4411092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Santiago Milestone – Km 60&lt;/a&gt; marks an important point on the Portuguese Way.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Caldas de Reis?
A: The hiking trails in Caldas de Reis are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 370 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse natural landscapes, the serene atmosphere along the rivers, and the beauty of the Fervenza de Segade waterfall. The mix of easy and more challenging routes also makes it appealing to a wide range of outdoor enthusiasts.

Q: Is Caldas de Reis a good destination for hikers interested in the Camino de Santiago?
A: Yes, Caldas de Reis is a significant and well-known stop on the Portuguese Way of the Camino de Santiago. The Camino itself offers numerous routes through the area, providing varied terrains including natural paths, dirt tracks, and paved sections. It's a natural hub for pilgrims and hikers looking to experience this historic journey.

Q: Are there any botanical gardens or tranquil green spaces for leisurely walks?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a more leisurely outdoor experience, the Botanical Garden and Carballeira (oak grove) in Caldas de Reis are ideal. These areas, recognized as Assets of Cultural Interest, offer a tranquil retreat with diverse plant species and well-maintained walking paths, perfect for a relaxing stroll.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Caldas de Reis?
A: Caldas de Reis offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, making them ideal. Summer can be warm, but the river trails and forested areas offer shade. Winters are typically mild, though some paths might be wetter.

Q: Are there any facilities or attractions related to the thermal waters in Caldas de Reis?
A: Caldas de Reis is renowned for its thermal waters, which have been utilized since Roman times. After a day of hiking, visitors can relax in the town's spa facilities or enjoy the outdoor thermal pools located next to the river. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4464057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caldas de Reis Thermal Baths&lt;/a&gt; are a key attraction.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in the Caldas de Reis area?
A: There are over 25 hiking routes available in the Caldas de Reis area, catering to various skill levels. This includes 13 easy routes, 8 moderate routes, and 5 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for every hiker.
tips: A magical stone bridge arched over the river landscape, surrounded by dense forests and cascading water around you.
A beautiful walk through the Caldas Reservoir and the landscape of the Umia River. Explore the lush green forest and fertile fields that surround this aquatic landscape.
